mnemonics: P.I.N. Game: Players, Interests, and iNteractions are the core components of a strategic game.
N.E.S.T. Your Strategy: A Nash Equilibrium is a Stable Tactic where no player wants to change their move.
keyPoints: A strategic game is a model of interaction between rational players who each choose a plan of action (strategy).
The outcome of the game depends on the strategies chosen by all players, not just one.
A Nash Equilibrium is a key solution concept where each player's strategy is a best response to the strategies of the others.
commonMistakes: Assuming players act irrationally or against their own self-interest.
Confusing a dominant strategy (best no matter what others do) with a Nash Equilibrium strategy (best only when others' strategies are fixed).
Thinking a game must have a single, unique Nash Equilibrium; many games have multiple or none.
